terms.definition: pandas operation that splits a DataFrame into groups by key column(s), applies a function per group, and combines results (split-apply-combine).
A groupby reduction that returns one summary value (or row) per group, such as sum, mean, count, or a custom function.
A groupby operation that returns a Series aligned to the original row index—same length as input—broadcasting a per-group statistic to each member row.
pandas function to join two DataFrames on shared column(s) or index, controlled by the how parameter (inner, left, right, outer).
Merge that keeps only rows whose join keys appear in both tables; unmatched keys from either side are dropped.
Merge that keeps all rows from the left table and attaches matching columns from the right; unmatched left keys get NaN for right columns.
Feature contamination when merges or groupby statistics incorporate information from held-out, future, or label data unavailable at prediction time.
